* Act as a trusted partner to VPs, Directors, and individual contributors understanding their challenges and co-creating solutions.
* Work hand-in-hand with Product and Product Marketing to ensure enablement content is accurate, relevant, and commercially sharp.
* Report on programme impact, adoption, and outcomes to the VP of RevOps and senior leadership.


**Please ensure you upload your CV in English**
 At nShift we believe in embracing diversity in all forms and fostering an inclusive environment for everyone which we believe is essential for our continued success. We're an equal-opportunity employer which means that all applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to ethnicity, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, family or parental status, national origin, veteran, neurodiversity, or disability status.

title: Sommarjobb! Transportadministratör till UPS i Arlandastad
description:
**Before you apply to a job, select your language preference from the options available at the top right of this page.**
 Explore your next opportunity at a Fortune Global 500 organization. Envision innovative possibilities, experience our rewarding culture, and work with talented teams that help you become better every day. We know what it takes to lead UPS into tomorrow—people with a unique combination of skill + passion. If you have the qualities and drive to lead yourself or teams, there are roles ready to cultivate your skills and take you to the next level.
   

  

**Job Description**
 Om jobbet
   

  

 UPS Arlanda söker efter Transportadministratör / Center Clerk för sommaren 2026. Rollen innehåller många olika arbetsuppgifter där du kommer arbeta både självständigt och i team, vi söker därför efter dig som är en lagspelare som bidrar med positiv stämning!
   

  

**Huvudsakliga Ansvarsområden**
 Som Transportadministratör / Center Clerk hjälper du chaufförer att lösa problem som kan uppstå i samband med paketleveranser eller hämtningar. Det kan till exempel handla om vägbeskrivningar eller portkoder. Du kommer även följa upp exportens arbete och kontakta kunder för eventuell saknad information. Utöver detta kommer du att ha hand om förlorade och skadade paket (tracers & damages) samt uppföljning och registrering av leveranser. I arbetet ingår även rapportering och analyser gällande vår service och kvalitetsmål.
   

  

 Arlanda är ett litet center där alla kross-tränas för att kunna täcka upp vid behov där vi söker efter dig som trivs med att vara flexibel och även hjälpa till på andra tjänster, exempelvis kan du få stötta morgonpersonal i hantering av paket och få hantera olika arbetsuppgifter parallellt.
   

  

**Vi Söker Dig Som**
* Har ett intresse för service och god ansvarskänsla
* Är noggrann och flexibel
* Har goda kunskaper i svenska och engelska, i både tal och skrift
* Kan arbeta självständigt i ett tidvis högt tempo
* Har goda datakunskaper, framförallt i Officepaketet


**Upplysningar Om Tjänsten**
* Tjänsten är tidsbegränsad till månaderna slutet av maj till augusti.
* Arbetsplats: Stockholm, Arlanda
* Arbetstid: Kontorstid med 40 timmars arbetsvecka, Måndag-Fredag 8:00-17:00
* Kollektivavtal finns med Unionen.


 Då vi är ett säkerhetsklassat företag så kommer vi att begära in utdrag ur belastningsregistret.
   

  

 Intervjuer sker löpande så vänta inte med att skicka in din ansökan!
   

  

**Employee Type**
 Fixed Term (Fixed Term)
   

  

 UPS is committed to providing a workplace free of discrimination, harassment, and retaliation.

title: Senior Consultant - Advanced .NET, C#, Azure, React, Power Platform, Office 365, AI
description:
**Job Title: Senior Consultant / Lead Engineer – Full Stack & Microsoft Technologies**  

**Location:** Sweden  

**Experience:** Minimum 7 years  

**Technologies:** Advanced .NET, C#, Azure, React, Power Platform, Office 365, AI **Role Overview**  

A highly skilled Senior Consultant with strong expertise across Microsoft technology stack and modern web frameworks. The role involves designing and delivering scalable, secure, and intelligent enterprise applications leveraging .NET, Azure, React, Power Platform, Office 365, and AI capabilities.  

The ideal candidate will work closely with business stakeholders, architects, and cross‑functional teams to deliver high‑impact digital transformation solutions. **Key Responsibilities**  

**Application Development**  

* Design, develop, and maintain enterprise applications using **C#, .NET Core / .NET 6+**, and **React**
* Build RESTful APIs, microservices, and backend services following clean architecture principles
* Develop responsive, high‑performance front‑end interfaces using **React, TypeScript, HTML, CSS**

**Microsoft Azure**  

* Architect and implement cloud‑native solutions using **Azure App Services, Functions, Logic Apps, Azure SQL, Cosmos DB, Storage Accounts**
* Implement **Azure DevOps CI/CD pipelines**, infrastructure as code, and monitoring
* Ensure security, scalability, availability, and cost optimization of Azure solutions

**Power Platform**  

* Design and develop solutions using **Power Apps (Canvas & Model‑Driven)**, **Power Automate**, **Power BI**, and **Dataverse**
* Integrate Power Platform with Azure services, .NET applications, and external APIs
* Establish governance, environment strategy, and ALM best practices

**Office 365 & Integrations**  

* Build solutions integrated with **Microsoft 365**, including **SharePoint Online, Teams, Outlook, OneDrive**
* Customize and extend Office 365 using SPFx, Graph APIs, and Power Platform
* Support automation and collaboration solutions across M365 workloads

**Artificial Intelligence**  

* Incorporate **AI/ML capabilities** such as Azure AI services, Cognitive Services, Copilot, or OpenAI integrations
* Design intelligent workflows for prediction, document processing, chatbots, and decision automation
* Ensure responsible AI usage with focus on security, compliance, and ethical standards

**Leadership & Collaboration**  

* Provide technical leadership, code reviews, and mentoring to junior team members
* Participate in architecture discussions and solution design reviews
* Collaborate with product owners, business analysts, and stakeholders to gather and refine requirements
* Support production systems, troubleshoot issues, and drive continuous improvements

 **Required Skills & Experience**  

* Minimum 7 years of hands‑on IT experience
* Strong expertise in C#, .NET Core / .NET Framework
* Proficiency in React.js, modern JavaScript/TypeScript
* Solid experience with Microsoft Azure cloud services
* Hands‑on experience with Power Platform (Power Apps, Power Automate, Power BI, Dataverse)
* Strong understanding of Office 365 / Microsoft 365 ecosystem
* Experience integrating AI services into enterprise applications
* Knowledge of REST APIs, microservices, and event‑driven architectures
* Exposure to Agile/Scrum development methodologies
* Excellent problem‑solving, communication, and stakeholder‑management skills

 **Nice‑to‑Have Skills**  

* Experience with **Dynamics 365**
* Knowledge of Kubernetes, Docker, or containerized deployments
* Experience with SPFx and Microsoft Graph
* Prior experience in consulting or client‑facing roles

 **Qualifications & Certifications**  

* Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or related field
* Preferred Microsoft Certifications:
  

	+ Azure Developer / Architect
	+ Power Platform Developer or Solution Architect
	+ AI or Data‑related Microsoft certifications

**About Infosys**  

Infosys is a global leader in next-generation digital services and consulting. We enable clients in 56 countries to navigate their digital transformation. With over three decades of experience in managing the systems and workings of global enterprises, we expertly steer our clients through the many next of their digital journey. We do it by enabling the enterprise with an AI-powered core that helps prioritize the execution of change. We also empower the business with agile digital at scale to deliver unprecedented levels of performance and customer delight. Our always-on learning agenda drives their continuous improvement through building and transferring digital skills, expertise and ideas from our innovation ecosystem.  

Visit **www.infosys.com** to see how Infosys can help your enterprise navigate your next.  

  

**All aspects of employment at Infosys are based on merit, competence and performance. We are committed to embracing diversity and creating an inclusive environment for all employees. Infosys is proud to be an equal opportunity employer**

title: Test Automation Engineer
description:
About Consilium Safety Group
  
At Consilium Safety Group, we don’t just build technology—we create solutions that protect people, assets, and the planet. As a global leader in fire and gas safety, we serve critical industries such as marine, energy, rolling stock, and infrastructure.
  
With more than 100 years of expertise, we combine deep industry knowledge with cutting-edge innovation to shape the future of Safety Tech. Headquartered in Gothenburg, Sweden, with over 55 offices worldwide, we are a fast-evolving global organization backed by Antin Infrastructure Partners. With strong financial support and a clear strategic vision, we are continuing an ambitious journey of growth and transformation—investing in innovation, operational excellence, and talent.
  
This is an exciting time to join us.
  
  

Your Role
  
As a Test Automation Engineer, you will play a central role in ensuring the quality and reliability of a complex, safety-critical system. This is a hands-on position where you will take ownership of building a complete automated testing ecosystem, from defining strategy and selecting tools to implementing frameworks and writing test code.
  
You will work across the full stack, covering frontend, backend, and system-level validation. Your work will include automated UI testing, backend and API verification, and system-level testing supported by simulations and realistic environments. A key part of the role is shaping how testing is done, defining what to test, how to test it, and how to ensure quality end-to-end across the product.
  
You will collaborate closely with developers and system engineers to build testable, scalable solutions, while integrating continuous testing into CI/CD pipelines. Beyond implementation, you will also help identify gaps in test coverage, improve processes, and contribute to a strong quality culture within the team.
  
  

What You Bring
  
We believe you have solid experience working with test automation in production environments and are comfortable writing code as part of your daily work, whether in TypeScript, C#, Python, or a similar language. You have hands-on experience with modern frontend testing tools such as Playwright, Cypress, or Selenium, as well as experience testing backend services and APIs.
  
You understand how automated testing fits into CI/CD workflows and are familiar with tools such as GitHub Actions or similar platforms. Just as importantly, you have the ability to think strategically about testing in complex, distributed systems and enjoy taking ownership of quality from a holistic perspective.
  
Experience with safety-critical or industrial systems is a plus, as is familiarity with simulators, virtual devices, or hardware-in-the-loop testing. Knowledge of performance or load testing and a broader full-stack understanding will also be valuable in this role.
  
  

Our Tech Stack
  
You will be working in an environment that includes TypeScript and Vue.js on the frontend, and C#/.NET with ASP.NET on the backend. Communication is handled through technologies such as gRPC and OPC-UA, and testing is primarily done using Playwright alongside internal tools. Our development practices are built around CI/CD pipelines, automated workflows, and DevOps principles.
  
  

Why Join Us?
  
This is an opportunity to build the testing foundation for software that has a direct impact on safety. You will have the chance to take real ownership and shape how automated testing is approached across the team, influencing tools, frameworks, and overall architecture.
  
You will be part of a highly dedicated engineering team working on a mission-critical product, where quality truly matters. Together, we are building what we aim to be the world’s most trusted Safety Management System.
  
  

Apply Now
  
Does this sound like your next challenge?
  
Please apply as soon as possible, as we review applications on a continuous basis.
  
Consilium Safety Group is an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity and inclusion.
  
  

Ready to learn more about our journey? Click to hear from our CEO!
  
Philip Isell Lind af Hageby, Consilium, en mästare på turnarounds - Värdeskaparna | En podd om riskkapital av OPX Partners | Podcast on Spotify

title: Test Consultant – Automation Focus
description:
At CGI, we are looking for a Test Consultant with strong automation skills to join our growing team. You will work closely with clients across industries, helping them improve quality assurance practices and accelerate delivery through modern test automation and RPA solutions.  

As a consultant, you will combine hands-on technical expertise with advisory capabilities, supporting clients in building scalable, efficient, and reliable testing solutions.

**Your future duties and responsibilities**


Design, implement, and maintain automated test frameworks and scripts  

* Develop and maintain automation using tools such as UiPath and other test automation frameworks
* Collaborate with cross-functional teams to define test strategies and acceptance criteria
* Integrate automated tests into CI/CD pipelines
* Execute functional, regression, and integration testing
* Analyze test results, identify defects, and support resolution
* Advise clients on QA and automation best practices, including RPA solutions
* Contribute to continuous improvement within CGI’s testing community
**Required qualifications to be successful in this role**


Experience in software testing and test automation  

* Hands-on experience with UiPath (RPA) and at least one test automation tool (e.g., Selenium, Cypress, Playwright)
* Programming skills in Java, JavaScript, Python, or C#
* Experience working with CI/CD tools (e.g., Azure DevOps, Jenkins, GitHub Actions)
* Good understanding of Agile methodologies
* Strong communication skills in English (Swedish is a plus)


Desired Competencies  

* Experience with API testing (e.g., Postman, REST Assured)
* Knowledge of cloud platforms (Azure, AWS, or GCP)
* ISTQB certification or equivalent


What You Can Expect from Us  

* A dynamic and inclusive work environment
* Opportunities to work with leading clients and technologies
* Continuous learning and certification support (including UiPath)
* Career development within a global organization
* Flexible working arrangements


Ready to become part of our success story?  

Apply now and join CGI in shaping the future of digital transformation.  

We look forward to hearing from you! Applications will be reviewed continuously, and the position may be filled before the final application deadline. For further information about this position, please contact the Hiring Manager Niriksha Shetty niriksha.shetty@cgi.com**Together, as owners, let’s turn meaningful insights into action.**  

  

Life at CGI is rooted in ownership, teamwork, respect and belonging. Here, you’ll reach your full potential because…  

  

You are invited to be an owner from day 1 as we work together to bring our Dream to life. That’s why we call ourselves CGI Partners rather than employees. We benefit from our collective success and actively shape our company’s strategy and direction.  

  

Your work creates value. You’ll develop innovative solutions and build relationships with teammates and clients while accessing global capabilities to scale your ideas, embrace new opportunities, and benefit from expansive industry and technology expertise.  

  

You’ll shape your career by joining a company built to grow and last. You’ll be supported by leaders who care about your health and well-being and provide you with opportunities to deepen your skills and broaden your horizons.
  

  

Come join our team—one of the largest IT and business consulting services firms in the world.

title: Erfaren testledare
description:
På Ductus får du som testledare en viktig roll i att säkerställa kvalitet i våra kunders mest centrala system. Med din erfarenhet bidrar du till att skapa struktur, tydlighet och framdrift i testarbetet, samtidigt som du är med och påverkar både arbetssätt och leverans.


Vilka är vi?


Tillsammans med kompetenta och engagerade kollegor får du hos oss vara med och bidra till vår vision om en mer hållbar värld, genom att utveckla digitala lösningar för våra kunder. Som ett teknikoberoende IT-konsultbolag kan vi erbjuda en bredd av varierande och utvecklande uppdrag. Självklart erbjuder vi också goda arbetsvillkor och en hälsosam balans mellan arbete och fritid.


Vi är idag cirka 80 medarbetare på Uppsalakontoret och drygt 300 medarbetare världen över, med kontor i Skellefteå, Stockholm, Uppsala, Malmö, Luleå, Boston, Longmont, Dallas, Singapore/Indonesien.


Om rollen


Hos oss arbetar vi som IT-konsulter och sätter stort värde på det vi bidrar med i våra uppdrag. I konsultrollen får du möjlighet att arbeta med olika typer av kunder, projekt, leveranser och tekniker. Tillsammans med din konsultchef avgör ni vilka uppdrag som passar dig bäst, utifrån var du befinner dig just nu, både personligen och i din karriär, samt vilken riktning du vill utvecklas i. Du kommer att vara en del av vårt affärsområde IT Solutions, där vi utvecklar och förvaltar IT-lösningar för företag, organisationer och myndigheter. I ditt arbete samarbetar du ofta i team tillsammans med kollegor från Ductus, kunden och ibland även andra konsultbolag.


Som testledare förväntas du ta ett ledande ansvar för testarbetet i uppdrag hos kund, där du planerar, koordinerar och följer upp testaktiviteter genom hela utvecklingsprocessen. Du säkerställer kvalitet i leveranser genom att etablera teststrategier, arbetssätt och en tydlig struktur för testarbetet.


Uppdragen omfattar ofta komplexa och verksamhetskritiska system, där du arbetar nära projektledare, utvecklare och verksamhet för att säkerställa att lösningarna uppfyller både funktionella och icke-funktionella krav. Arbetet sker ofta i agila team med stöd av verktyg som Azure DevOps, Jira eller liknande, där även testautomatisering kan förekomma.


Du bör ha:


* Minst 6 års erfarenhet av rollen som testare.
* Minst 2 års erfarenhet av rollen som testledare.
* God förståelse av agila utvecklingsmetoder och testarens roll i dessa.
* God förmåga att tillgodogöra sig teknisk dokumentation.
* Mycket god svenska och engelska i både tal och skrift.

Meriterande:


* ISTQB-certifiering (Test Manager).
* Erfarenhet av kravfångst för framtagande av egenutvecklade it-system.
* Erfarenhet av att leda workshops.
* Erfarenhet av verktyg såsom Azure DevOps, Jira eller Confluence.
* Erfarenhet av testautomatisering.
* Kunskap inom SQL.

Vem söker vi?


Vi letar efter dig som har ett intresse för teknik, människor och hållbar utveckling. Du är en ödmjuk och ansvarstagande lagspelare med en stark nyfikenhet, som gärna både lär av andra och delar med dig av din egen kunskap. Att möta kunder och kollegor på ett vänligt, nyfiket och professionellt sätt ser du som en självklarhet. Dessa egenskaper speglar vår värdegrund, som vi sammanfattar i vårt Ductus-hjärta: Pålitlighet, Samarbete, Empati och Innovation.


På Ductus är vi övertygade om att en inkluderande och diversifierad arbetsplats ger bäst förutsättningar att möta våra kunders behov. Vi strävar efter att spegla samhällets mångfald och därför välkomnar vi alla som känner igen sig i vår värdegrund.


Vad erbjuder vi?


Hos oss får du en arbetsplats där du får utrymme att växa. Vi erbjuder alltid löpande kompetensutveckling som du själv är med och påverkar och du har tillgång till företagets samlade kunskapsbas som stöd i ditt arbete. Din närmaste chef kommer att stötta dig genom regelbundna 1:1-samtal där ni stämmer av läget.


För oss är gemenskap och sammanhållning viktig. Förutom att vi har väldigt roligt tillsammans på jobbet så arrangerar vi också sociala aktiviteter utanför arbetstid för de som kan och vill.


Vi vet att en trevlig arbetsmiljö, balans i livet och god hälsa är en förutsättning för att du ska må bra och nå din fulla potential. Vårt kontor i Uppsala är centralt beläget och vi erbjuder flexibla arbetstider och bra förmåner som exempelvis kollektivavtal och friskvårdsbidrag.


Om tjänsten


Ort: Uppsala  

Omfattning: Heltid  

Tillträde: Enligt överenskommelse


För mer information


Kontakta gärna Ida Morota på vårt Uppsala-kontor om du vill veta mer om företaget eller möjligheten att jobba på Ductus. Du när Ida enklast på mejl: Ida.Morota@ductus.se


Ansökan


Välkommen med din ansökan via www.ductus.com/careers.
  

Urval sker löpande, så skicka in din ansökan idag!

title: Laboratorieingenjör till Enheten för datavetenskap
description:
Tillsvidareanställning
  
  

Heltid
  
  

senast 2026-05-29
  
  

**Ref nr:** ORU 2.1.1-02186/2026
  
  

Vi söker en laboratorieingenjör för tillsvidareanställning vid Institutionen för naturvetenskap och teknik.
  
  

Bakgrund
  
  

Enheten för datavetenskap bedriver forskning och utbildning inom datavetenskap med fokus på AI, robotik och cybersäkerhet. Enheten har vuxit kraftigt och behöver stärkas med en ny laboratorieingenjör. I ett ämne som ställer krav på specialbyggda och välskötta hårdvarulösningar och mjukvarumiljöer är en laboratorieingenjör helt central för att både undervisning och forskning ska fungera väl. Vi söker därför en engagerad och mångsidig laboratorieingenjör som vill spela en nyckelroll i vår verksamhet.
  
  

Tjänsten spänner över flera kompetensområden. Vi värdesätter framför allt mekatronisk kompetens (elektronik, mekanik och mjukvaruteknik), men välkomnar även sökande med bakgrund inom mjukvaruutveckling eller datahantering.
  
  

Vi erbjuder en varierad roll med stort eget ansvar i en stimulerande forskningsmiljö. Du kommer att arbeta nära forskning och undervisning inom robotik och AI, tillsammans med engagerade och kompetenta kollegor. För en översikt av universitetets satsningar inom dessa områden, se https://www.oru.se/ai .
  
Arbetsuppgifter
  
  

Som laboratorieingenjör hos oss har du ett brett och varierat uppdrag. Du är den person som kollegor vänder sig till när de behöver konstruera specialanpassade lösningar inom elektronik, mekanik eller mjukvara. Du planerar och prioriterar underhållsarbeten i samråd med forskar- och lärargruppen. Du ansvarar också för säkerheten i labbmiljön. Det innebär praktiskt arbete med elektriska och mekaniska komponenter, konstruktion av skräddarsydda lösningar, samt underhåll av de robotplattformar som används i undervisning och forskning, både förebyggande och vid akuta behov. Du är också ansvarig för att vår labbmiljö fungerar väl som forsknings- och utbildningsmiljö. Arbetsuppgifterna inkluderar också att samordna och strukturera repositorier för mjukvara och data, säkerställa att data uppfyller FAIR-kraven (findable, accessible, interoperable, reusable) samt dokumentera dataflöden och metadata i samarbete med forskare.
  
Kvalifikationer
  
  

Den primära tekniska kompetensen vi söker är inom elektronik och mekanik, inbyggda system och robotik.
  
  

Som meriterande ser vi kompetens inom mjukvaruutveckling och DevOps. Vi värdesätter också kompetens inom datahantering.
  
  

För tjänsten krävs relevant ingenjörsutbildning eller likvärdig dokumenterad erfarenhet. Du ska ha praktisk erfarenhet av elektriska och mekaniska system, samt förmåga att självständigt felsöka och lösa tekniska problem. God förmåga att strukturera och prioritera arbete i en dynamisk miljö är ett krav.
  
  

Tidigare erfarenhet från en teknisk forsknings- eller utvecklingsmiljö är starkt meriterande. Som meriterande räknas även erfarenhet av programmering, särskilt med ROS, Arduino eller liknande plattformar. Det är också meriterande med kunskaper inom mjukvaruförvaltning (GitHub, GitLab, CI/CD-flöden) samt kännedom om FAIR-principerna och hantering av forskningsdata.
  
  

Du behöver kunna kommunicera obehindrat på engelska, medan god kommunikationsförmåga på svenska i tal och skrift är meriterande.
  
Information
  
  

Anställningen är på heltid (100 %) och tillsvidare. Örebro universitet tillämpar individuell lönesättning och i förekommande fall provanställning.
  
  

Närmare upplysningar om anställningen lämnas av enhetschef Martin Magnusson, 019-30 38 70, e-post: martin.magnusson@oru.se eller prefekt Henrik Olsson, 019-30 12 36, e-post: henrik.olsson@oru.se .
  
  

Vid Örebro universitet förväntar vi oss att alla medarbetare är öppna för utveckling och förändring, tar ansvar för sin egen arbetsinsats och kvalitet, visar engagemang att samverka och bidra till utveckling samt visar respekt för andra genom ett konstruktivt och professionellt förhållningssätt.
  
  

Örebro universitet arbetar aktivt med lika villkor och jämställdhet och för att vår arbetsmiljö ska präglas av öppenhet, tillit och respekt. Vi värdesätter de kvaliteter mångfald tillför verksamheten.
  
an
  
  

En komplett ansökan ska innehålla följande elektroniska dokument:
  
  

Ett personligt brev med motivering till ditt intresse av anställningen som laboratorieingenjör
  
  

CV med relevant beskrivning av samlad kompetens
  
  

Kopior av relevanta betyg/intyg som styrker din kompetens
  
  

Endast handlingar skrivna på svenska, engelska, norska eller danska kan bedömas.
  
  

Sista ansökningsdag är 2026-05-29. Välkommen med din ansökan!
  
  

Då vi i samband med den här rekryteringen redan har gjort våra val vad gäller externa samarbetspartners och marknadsföring undanber vi oss kontakt med rekryteringsföretag och annonssäljare.
  
  

Enligt Riksarkivets föreskrifter måste vi arkivera en omgång ansökningshandlingar exkl. publikationer i två år efter att tillsättningsbeslutet vunnit laga kraft.

title: DevSecOps engineer to API Governance team | SEB, Solna
description:
Are you passionate about new technology and making a difference? Join API Governance team!
   

  

 SEB is more than just a bank with an IT function. We are a progressive tech company powered by innovation and fueled by collaboration. From Java to AI and everything in between, we offer you an impressive tech stack and several technology paths. Together we're innovating for a sustainable society and building the tech arena of the future, a welcoming space for everyone.
   

  

**About the role:**
 Join a team of developers who are shaping a secure and reliable API platform at scale. You’ll take ownership of the API Gateway, ensuring it evolves with the latest upgrades and security standards. You’ll get hands-on with Kubernetes and OpenShift, improve and run services on GCP, and build modern infrastructure with Terraform. It’s a chance to work with cutting-edge tech, solve real challenges, and make a direct impact on how our business runs.
   

  

**Your responsibilities will include:**
* Looking after our API Gateway, keeping it secure, up to date, and running smoothly.
* Building and automating cloud infrastructure using Terraform (we love clean, repeatable deployments).
* Deploying, monitoring, and tuning workloads across Kubernetes and OpenShift.
* Running and improving services across our cloud environments (GCP, but also OpenShift/Kubernetes and other cloud tooling).
* Creating tools and small services that make our operations easier and smarter.
* Ensuring security, compliance, and good governance are naturally built into everything we do.
* Enhancing our observability using Splunk, cloud logging/monitoring, Prometheus, and similar tools.


**What helps you thrive here:**
* You’re comfortable working in at least one modern programming language. We use Go, but Java, Python, C#, and others are just as welcome.
* You have experience with API Gateway configuration, upgrades, and security.
* You know your way around Terraform and infrastructure-as-code workflows.
* You’ve worked with Kubernetes and/or OpenShift.
* You understand cloud platforms such as GCP, AWS, or Azure.
* Linux troubleshooting feels natural to you.
* You’ve used observability tools like Splunk, Prometheus, Grafana, or ELK.
* Most importantly, you’re approachable, collaborative, and someone who makes the team better, not just the tech.


**What we offer:**
* Extensive training and learning opportunities.
* Friendly and welcoming culture.
* Innovative company in forefront of technology.
* Opportunities to help transform an industry.
* Mobility within a large global company.
* Agile and modern ways of working.
* Empowering environment.
* Deliver world-class service to customers.


**Do you want to be a part of SEB?**
 It is our fundamental belief that inclusion and diversity is crucial for our future success. We strive to have an inclusive, value-driven culture where employees feel valued, respected, and involved irrespective of who they are, what they believe or where they come from.
   

  

 Since we select candidates continuously, feel free to send in your application today via the link in this ad, but no later than 20th of April. If you have questions about the position, please contact anton.hanni@seb.se, but we will not process applications via email.
   

  

 Please be aware that our final candidates undergo background checks, a process that includes for example identity control, verification of qualifications, credit checks, company engagements and history of crime. In some cases, we also apply random drug checks. During employment, employees within SEB can also expect to undergo recurring background checks.
   

  

 SEB Sweden has a redeployment responsibility, why this position might be covered by internal redeployment
   

  

 Learn more about working at SEB on our Career website or through our Career podcasts.
   

  

 sebgroup.com/career
   

  

 bit.ly/SEBcareer

title: Software Engineer / DevOps
description:
**About Applied Intuition**
 Applied Intuition, Inc. is powering the future of physical AI. Founded in 2017 and now valued at $15 billion, the Silicon Valley company is creating the digital infrastructure needed to bring intelligence to every moving machine on the planet. Applied Intuition services the automotive, defense, trucking, construction, mining and agriculture industries in three core areas: tools and infrastructure, operating systems, and autonomy. Eighteen of the top 20 global automakers, as well as the United States military and its allies, trust the company’s solutions to deliver physical intelligence. Applied Intuition is headquartered in Sunnyvale, California, with offices in Washington, D.C.; San Diego; Ft. Walton Beach, Florida; Ann Arbor, Michigan; London; Stuttgart; Munich; Stockholm; Bangalore; Seoul; and Tokyo. Learn more at applied.co.
   

  

*We are an in-office company, and our expectation is that employees primarily work from their Applied Intuition office 5 days a week. However, we also recognize the importance of flexibility and trust our employees to manage their schedules responsibly. This may include occasional remote work, starting the day with morning meetings from home before heading to the office, or leaving earlier when needed to accommodate family commitments.*
**About The Role**
 We are looking for a software engineer with a passion for improving developer experience and deployment processes. In this role, you will work across engineering teams to identify key sources of friction, improve workflows, develop new tools, and generally uplevel the velocity and reliability of the entire software, and automotive development and deployment lifecycle. Please note that this role involves travel to our customer site on a weekly basis.
   

  

**At Applied Intuition, you will:**
* Design, build, and improve the tools, libraries, and frameworks for our engineering team
* Build and test scalable tools for deploying to various automotive and cloud platforms
* Optimize our compile/build times using tools like Bazel, CMake, Docker
* Improve software deployment and data collection frameworks
* Identify and solve bottlenecks within our software stack


**We're looking for someone who has:**
* 5+ years of relevant experience
* A Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or equivalent
* Experience with Cloud Infrastructure
* Experience using Python or C++
* Experience with CI/CD, build, and deployment technologies like Bazel, Buildkite, Docker, Kubernetes, and Jenkins, and comfortability with scripting for automation
* Experience with Linux and/or ARM systems
* Experience working with configuration management tools like Ansible and Terraform


**Nice to have:**
* Experience using Go, Java, or Kotlin
* Experience working in automotive or related industries
* Experience with deploying software on either public clouds (e.g., AWS) or on-premise clusters


**Benefits and** 
**perks**
* Lunch and dinner allowance
* Fitness (600 USD) and learning stipends (1,000 USD) annually
* Equity in the company


 Compensation at Applied Intuition for eligible roles includes base salary, equity, and benefits. Base salary is a single component of the total compensation package, which may also include equity in the form of options and/or restricted stock units, comprehensive health, dental, vision, life and disability insurance coverage, learning and wellness stipends, and paid time off. Note that benefits are subject to change and may vary based on jurisdiction of employment.
   

  

 Applied Intuition pay ranges reflect the minimum and maximum intended target base salary for new hire salaries for the position. The actual base salary offered to a successful candidate will additionally be influenced by a variety of factors including experience, credentials & certifications, educational attainment, skill level requirements, interview performance, and the level and scope of the position.
   

  

 Please reference the job posting’s subtitle for where this position will be located. For pay transparency purposes, the base salary range for this full-time position in the location listed is: 511.200 - 1.098.200 SEK yearly.
   

  

*Don’t meet every single requirement? If you’re excited about this role but your past experience doesn’t align perfectly with every qualification in the job description, we encourage you to apply anyway. You may be just the right candidate for this or other roles.*
*Applied Intuition is an equal opportunity employer and federal contractor or subcontractor. Consequently, the parties agree that, as applicable, they will abide by the requirements of 41 CFR 60-1.4(a), 41 CFR 60-300.5(a) and 41 CFR 60-741.5(a) and that these laws are incorporated herein by reference. These regulations prohibit discrimination against qualified individuals based on their status as protected veterans or individuals with disabilities, and prohibit discrimination against all individuals based on their race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or national origin. These regulations require that covered prime contractors and subcontractors take affirmative action to employ and advance in employment individuals without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, protected veteran status or disability. The parties also agree that, as applicable, they will abide by the requirements of Executive Order 13496 (29 CFR Part 471, Appendix A to Subpart A), relating to the notice of employee rights under federal labor laws.*

title: DevOps Engineer
description:
*Internetstiftelsen har ett samhällsviktigt uppdrag – vi ser till att internet i Sverige fungerar genom vårt ansvar för toppdomänen .se och driften av .nu, vi driver identitets- och behörighetsfederationer inom skola, vård och omsorg och vi folkbildar i frågor om internet och digitalisering.* 




  





 Nu söker vi en DevOps Engineer som vill vara med och bygga, drifta och utveckla robusta system som används av hela samhället.
 



  





**Om rollen** 




 Hos oss arbetar du med samhällskritiska system som måste fungera dygnet runt och är en del av Sveriges digitala infrastruktur. Du kommer vara med och bidra till arkitektur och tekniska vägval, samt själv arbeta operativt med systemen i produktion, inklusive felsökning och löpande förbättringar, med fokus på att säkerställa stabil och skalbar drift i Kubernetes, både lokal- och molnmiljö.
 



  





 Du arbetar också med att stärka vår förmåga att förstå och förbättra systemen genom loggning, övervakning och analys, samtidigt som du driver automatisering och utveckling av infrastrukturen.
 



  





**Vem är du?** 




 Du trivs i en miljö där samarbete och kunskapsdelning är en självklar del av vardagen. Du tar ansvar för ditt arbete, driver uppgifter framåt och har ett lösningsorienterat förhållningssätt.
 



  





 Vi tror också att du är:
 


* Prestigelös och bidrar till en inkluderande arbetsmiljö
* Nyfiken och vill fortsätta utvecklas inom DevOps och infrastruktur
* Van vid att arbeta i både äldre och moderna system och kan få dem att fungera tillsammans i praktiken.



  





**Vi söker dig som har** 



* Minst 5 års erfarenhet inom DevOps eller liknande roller
* Goda kunskaper i Linux och Kubernetes
* Erfarenhet av Infrastructure as Code (t.ex. Terraform eller Ansible)



  





*Vi uppmuntrar dig att söka även om du inte uppfyller alla krav, det viktigaste för oss är din vilja att utvecklas och bidra. Vi värdesätter olika bakgrunder och perspektiv.* 




  





**Om Internetstiftelsen** 




*Internetstiftelsen är en oberoende, affärsdriven och allmännyttig organisation. Vi verkar för ett internet som bidrar positivt till människan och samhället.* 




  





*Vi ansvarar för den svenska toppdomänen .se och för driften av toppdomänen .nu. Intäkterna från affärsverksamheten finansierar en rad satsningar inom forskning och utbildning i syfte att internetanvändare i Sverige ska kunna vidareutveckla sin användning på ett medvetet sätt och att ge kunskap om internetanvändningen i Sverige och digitaliseringens påverkan på samhället.* 




  





*Exempel på våra satsningar är rapporten Svenskarna och Internet, folkbildningsinitiativet Internetkunskap, den öppna lärresursen Digitala lektioner, konferensen Internetdagarna och mätverktyget Bredbandskollen. Vår vision är att alla i Sverige vill, vågar och kan använda internet.* 




  





**Övrig information** 




 Vårt kontor ligger vid vattnet på Hammarby kaj 10 i Hammarby Sjöstad, Stockholm. Vi tillämpar en hybridmodell för vår arbetsvecka med möjlighet att jobba på distans vissa dagar. Tjänsten omfattar heltid och är en tillsvidareanställning med inledande provanställning.
 



 Tillträde så snart som möjligt med hänsyn till eventuell uppsägningstid.
 



  





 Urval sker löpande, så skicka din ansökan så snart som möjligt! Vid frågor kontakta holger.artman@internetstiftelsen.se 072-245 71 00.
 



  





**Fackliga kontaktpersoner** 




 Unionen
 



 Markus Rautiainen
 



 Nicklas Larsson
 



 E-post: unionen.internetstiftelsen@protonmail.com
 



  





 SACO
 



 Joakim Hagsköld
 



 E-post: joakim.hagskold@internetstiftelsen.se

title: Cloud Workspace Engineer – IAM & Cloud Platforms
description:
**HAYPP GROUP**
 We address one of the world’s biggest problems – how to end smoking. There are 1,1 billion smokers in the world. Around 8 million people die every year from smoking-related diseases. Our goal is to significantly reduce death rates, save lives and inspire people to choose smoke-free enjoyment. How? By offering people other nicotine products online.
   

  

 Haypp Group is one of the Nordics’ leading e-commerce groups with eleven store brands, present in seven countries where we serve more than 1 million active consumers. Through brands such as Snusbolaget.se, Northerner.com, Nicokick.com etc., we sell nicotine pouches, Swedish style snus online as well as disposable vapes. With our headquarters located on Östgötagatan in Stockholm we are 300 team members mainly but not exclusively based in Sweden. Also, we are an innovative and fast-moving company that is growing rapidly, driving sustainable business and are changing the world in the process. We are currently on an exciting expansion journey, converting more and more smokers to smoke-free alternatives across all our markets globally.
   

  

**THE ROLE**
 At Haypp Group, we’re evolving our IT environment to be smarter, faster, and more secure across multiple cloud platforms. We’re looking for a Cloud Engineer with strong IAM knowledge to help us build, manage, and improve secure cloud solutions across Microsoft Azure, AWS, and Google Cloud Platform.
   

  

 This role is ideal for someone who combines solid cloud engineering experience with a strong understanding of Identity and Access Management, federation, and cloud security. You will play a key role in strengthening our multicloud environment, improving secure access across platforms, and helping us build scalable and future-ready ways of working.
   

  

**What You'll Do**
 As a Cloud Engineer at Haypp Group, you will work hands-on with our cloud platforms and identity landscape. You will:
   

  

* Design, build, and maintain secure cloud solutions across Azure, AWS, and GCP
* Strengthen our IAM and identity provider strategy, especially around integrating Entra ID with AWS and GCP for SSO, MFA, and unified access
* Implement and improve identity federation, access governance, and role-based / least-privilege access models across cloud platforms
* Manage and improve access for users, groups, applications, and workloads across internal and third-party services
* Drive cloud and IT projects that improve scalability, compliance, resilience, and security
* Automate repetitive tasks and cloud workflows using tools such as PowerShell, Python, Bash, Terraform, or similar
* Partner with internal stakeholders to integrate new SaaS applications and cloud services with secure authentication and access controls
* Maintain and improve documentation, operational routines, troubleshooting guides, and disaster recovery playbooks
* Monitor cloud environments, support audits, and help implement best practices for security, compliance, and operational excellence


**What We're Looking For**
 We’re looking for someone with strong hands-on cloud engineering experience and a clear understanding of IAM in modern cloud environments. We believe you have experience with:
   

  

* Azure, AWS, and/or Google Cloud Platform, ideally with solid exposure to at least two of them
* IAM, SSO, MFA, and federation across cloud platforms
* Entra ID / Azure AD, AWS IAM, and ideally Google Cloud IAM
* Identity and authentication protocols such as SAML, OAuth2, and OpenID Connect
* Integrating cloud platforms and SaaS applications with central identity providers
* Scripting and automation for provisioning, reporting, and operational improvements
* Working with cloud security principles such as least privilege, conditional access, and Zero Trust
* Communicating clearly with both technical and non-technical stakeholders


**It’s a plus if you also have experience with:**
* Terraform or other infrastructure-as-code tools
* Workload identity and service-to-service authentication
* IT service management and asset management in multicloud environments
* Compliance or security frameworks such as ISO 27001, NIST, GDPR, NIS2, or DORA
* Microsoft 365, Google Workspace, SharePoint, or similar workplace platforms
* Relevant certifications in Azure, AWS, GCP, or Microsoft 365


**Examples Of Projects You May Work On**
* Integrating Entra ID with AWS and GCP to create a more seamless and secure access model
* Improving SSO and MFA across business-critical internal and third-party applications
* Migrating legacy systems and services into a more modern, cloud-first setup
* Rolling out stronger Zero Trust principles across our cloud environment
* Automating user lifecycle, access provisioning, and compliance reporting
* Supporting secure onboarding of new SaaS tools and business applications
* Improving disaster recovery and cloud resilience across multiple platforms


**You Will Thrive Here If You**
 We’re looking for someone who:
   

  

* Takes ownership and enjoys solving real business and technical challenges
* Has a strong quality and security mindset
* Is proactive and enjoys improving processes through automation
* Likes collaborating across teams and building trust with stakeholders
* Is curious and keeps up with modern cloud, IAM, and security practices
* Can balance hands-on delivery with long-term thinking


**What We Offer**
 We are a value driven company with our values
 *We team up* 
 ,
 *Innovation Drives Us* 
 ,
 *Inclusion and Non-Hierarchy* 
 and
 *Going the Extra Mile* 
 at the heartbeat of everything that we do. With us you can always expect to get help from people who want to achieve things together and to be able to speak your mind in every social constellation you are part of. We highly value all ideas no matter where they come from, and we are not afraid to try new things out. We are also welcoming, inclusive and everyone at Haypp Group has a voice. In addition, we seize opportunities by putting in hands-on, hard work and celebrating when we reach our goals. With us, you should always feel safe, appreciated and valued but at the same time challenged and excited.
   

  

**Benefits**
 Because we recognize that our team members are our most important differentiator, we offer our team members generous benefits. We offer the following perks to everyone at Haypp Group:
   

  

* Premium ITP1 occupational pension savings plan
* Insurance plan, including private healthcare
* 30 days of paid vacation
* Parental pay lift
* Wellness allowance of 5,000 SEK per year
* Lots of social happenings


**SEND US YOUR APPLICATION TODAY!**
 If this sounds like a place where you would want to contribute and grow, let us know you’re interested by submitting your application!
   

  

 Haypp Group is committed to equality and diversity, and we welcome applications from all qualified individuals regardless of ethnicity, religion, age, gender, sexual orientation, disability, and marital status. We want to offer you great recruitment experience, and if there is anything we can do to make you more comfortable in the process with us, please let us know.
   

  

 If you have any questions about the role or what it’s like to work at Haypp Group in general, feel free to reach out to our responsible recruiter Lea Sjösten at lea.sjosten@hayppgroup.com. We look forward to hearing from you!
   

  

 The Haypp Group is spearheading the global transformation from smoking to healthier product alternatives. With origins in Scandinavia our extensive experience from pioneering markets in smoke free alternatives, as well as being a leader in the e-commerce sector, we now fully bring our vision to a global scale.
   

  

 With 11 e-commerce brands, the Haypp Group is present in seven countries where we serve more than 1 100 000 consumers.

title: Senior Platform Engineer
description:
Vi har nu ett nytt behov och söker därför en konsult och kollega med nedan kvalifikationer. Tjänsten är en del av vår konsultverksamhet vilket innebär att du blir anställd av oss och arbetar hos kund alt. interna projekt och åtaganden.
   

  

**Din profil (Skall krav)**
* Flera års erfarenhet som Platform Engineer, Cloud Engineer eller DevOps Engineer.
* Djup teknisk kunskap om Google Cloud Platform, bl.a. Compute Engine, Cloud Storage, Kubernetes (GKE), Cloud Run, IAM och nätverksdesign.
* God vana vid Terraform och andra IaC-verktyg.
* Erfarenhet av CI/CD (t.ex. GitHub Actions, Jenkins, GitLab CI).
* Kunskap om containerteknik (Docker, Kubernetes).
* Erfarenhet av skript/programmering (t.ex. Python, Bash, Go).
* Förmåga att arbeta agilt i tvärfunktionella team.


**Meriterande**
* Erfarenhet av säkerhetsarkitektur och policies i GCP.
* Certifiering som Professional Cloud Architect eller motsvarande.
* Erfarenhet från större organisationer eller offentlig sektor.


**Sista ansökningdatum:** 
 10-04-2026
   

  

**Vi erbjuder dig följande, Valet är ditt!**
 🚀 Spännande uppdrag hos våra många kunder. Vi fokuserar på långsiktiga uppdrag, vilket ger dig möjlighet att skapa värde för kunden samtidigt som du utvecklas själv.
   

  

 🌟 En trygg månadslön – tillsammans bestämmer vi en grundlön baserad på dina uppdrag.
   

  

 💼 Tjänstepension och stora möjligheter till löneväxling.
   

  

 🌴 Semester - Önskar du mer än 30 dagar semester, kanske 60 dagar?
   

  

 🏥 Privat sjukvårdsförsäkring, olycksfallsförsäkring och livförsäkring för ökad trygghet.
   

  

 📚 Kompetensutveckling – du får själv sätta din budget och välja vilka utbildningar du vill delta i.
   

  

 🕑 Livskvalitet - Har du kanske svårt att få ihop pusslet i vardagen och önskar arbeta lite mindre och ändå kunna tjäna lika mycket om inte mer?
   

  

* Vi hanterar ansökningar löpande, med hänsyn till höga arbetstrycket är det extra viktigt med en kort motivering för tjänsten som förklarar hur man möter kraven.

title: Founding Engineer at Vantir | Stockholm
description:
**Who We Are** 




 Vantir is an AI-native consulting and venture-building firm based in Stockholm. We’re a group of passionate engineers and entrepreneurs on a mission to help businesses thrive in the modern age of AI.
 



  





 We’re just getting started, so joining us now means being part of something exciting, helping to build a company from the ground up and shaping its culture, direction, and future.
 



  





**What We’re Looking For** 




 We’re looking for talented AI/data/cloud professionals (engineers, analysts, scientist) and technical management consultants who love solving real-world problems using modern technologies. If you’re ready to work on cutting-edge projects with top-tier clients AND want the rare chance to become a founding member of a company, you’ve come to the right place.
 



  





 In this role, you’ll work closely with clients to design and implement data solutions that help them make smarter decisions. You’ll also help us define how we work and build a company culture that fosters learning, collaboration, and innovation.
 



  





**What’s in It for You?** 



* A rare opportunity to join a newly founded consulting firm and become a Founding Engineer.
* Work directly on high-impact projects with great clients.
* Opportunities to work with our venture-building business
* Be part of a collaborative team where your ideas matter and your work is valued.
* Help shape the technical direction and culture of a growing company.
* Competitive compensation and benefits.



  





**What We’d Love to See in You** 



* Master's Degree in Engineering or Technology
* At least 3 years of experience as a Data Engineer, Software Engineer, Data Scientist, AI Engineer, Technical Management Consultant or similar.
* Experience working with AWS, GCP or Azure.
* Strong skills in modern software development.
* Experience with tools like Snowflake or dbt (or similar modern data stack tools).
* A knack for creative problem-solving and a passion for using data to drive business decisions.
* You live in or near Stockholm



  





**Why Join Us?** 




 This is more than a job. It’s your chance to be part of something exciting and meaningful - not just for our clients but for our company, too. As a Founding Engineer, you’ll help establish Vantir’s reputation as an innovative data consulting powerhouse, while growing with us every step of the way.
 



  





 Ready to make your mark? Apply today and let’s build something great together.
 



 Learn more about the journey at vantir.se/careers

title: DevOps och DevSecOps-ingenjörer
description:
**Combitech söker seniora DevOps och DevSecOps-ingenjör till ett team i Stockholm. Vi erbjuder en spännande roll där du får möjlighet att arbeta som konsult hos någon av våra kunder inom bland annat försvarsindustrin. Du kommer jobba med de senaste teknologierna inom molntjänster och automatisering, främst AWS och Azure. Här får du chansen att växa och utvecklas tillsammans med oss, samtidigt som du bidrar till att forma framtidens säkra samhälle.**
**Rollen**
 I den här rollen får du möjlighet att:
   

  

* Automatisera och optimera utvecklings- och driftsprocesser – skapa effektiva flöden som förbättrar kvalitet, spårbarhet och leveranshastighet.
* Bygga och underhålla CI/CD-pipelines – med verktyg som GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, Circle CI, Azure Pipelines.
* Arbeta med containerisering och orkestrering – använda Kubernetes (EKS/GKE/AKS), Helm/Kustomize för att skapa skalbara och driftsäkra lösningar.
* Hantera infrastruktur som kod – med verktyg som Terraform, Pulumi och CloudFormation för att säkerställa stabil och säker drift.
* Säkerställa kvalitet och säkerhet – genom automatiska tester, kodgranskning, IAM-hantering och scanning av kod och containers.
* Jobba med monitorering och incidenthantering – med verktyg som Prometheus, Grafana och ELK-stack för att hålla systemen stabila.
* Integrera säkerhetslösningar, inklusive Vault eller molnbaserad secret-manager, SAST/DAST-pipeline, SBOM-generering och efterlevnadskontroller (SOC 2, ISO 27001)
* Samarbeta nära utvecklare, testare och driftsteam – för att skapa lösningar som är både skalbara och driftsäkra.


**Vi söker dig som**
* Har relevant utbildningsbakgrund : Ingenjörsutbildning eller annan teknisk utbildning inom mjukvara eller IT.
* Kan tekniker, verktyg och språk: Erfarenhet av CI/CD-verktyg som Azure DevOps, Jenkins, GitHub Actions eller Octopus Deploy samt containerisering med Docker och Kubernetes.
* Har erfarenhet av DevOps-arbete (minimum 5 år), arbete med molntjänster som AWS och Azure och gärna skriptning i Bash, PowerShell eller Python.
* Är analytisk och lösningsorienterad: Har förmåga att identifiera förbättringsmöjligheter och driva förändring.
* Har god samarbetsförmåga och kommunikationsförmåga: Trivs med att arbeta i team och skapa effektiva flöden tillsammans med utvecklare och driftteam.
* Är flexibel och positiv inför förändring: Har en vilja att utvecklas och bidra till förbättringar i processer och arbetssätt.


**Då tjänsten innebär arbete som omfattas av försvarssekretess krävs det att du genomför och godkänns i en säkerhetsprövning med registerkontroll.
   

  

 Urval sker löpande. Vi ser fram emot din ansökan!**
**Kom och gör skillnad**
 Vi tror att man gör ett bättre jobb om man kan fokusera på rätt saker, därför gör vi det lätt att kombinera jobbet med vardagen. Vi satsar på våra medarbetare, de utgör kärnan, hjärnan och hjärtat i vår verksamhet. Dessutom står vi på en stadig grund av högteknologi, forskning och anställningstrygghet med försvars- och säkerhetskoncernen Saab som ägare.
   

  

 Vill du veta mer om hur det är att arbeta hos oss? Kolla in våra karriärsidor!
   

  

**Kontaktperson/rekryterande chef:** 
 Therese Elwing, therese.elwing@combitech.com
   

  

 Vi undanber oss rekryteringssamarbeten och reklam.

title: QA specialist/Test automation engineer within Savings & Investment | SEB, Solna
description:
SEB is more than just a bank with an IT function. We are a progressive tech company powered by innovation and fuelled by collaboration. From COBOL to AI and everything in between, we offer you an impressive tech stack and several technology paths. Together we're innovating for a sustainable society and building the tech arena of the future, a welcoming space for everyone. Are you ready to take on a new challenge? If so, you'll be interested in this opportunity!
   

  

 Do you want to be part of an exciting journey developing SEB's future Savings & Investment solutions?
   

  

 Our customers have told us that our digital Savings and Investments-offering isn't good enough, and we are listening. Instead of a low ranking, we aim for first place in customer satisfaction. Our brilliant teams are working hard to achieve that, and now we are looking for a senior QA specialist/Test Automation Engineer who wants to join and is up for the challenge.
   

  

**About The Role**
 The team is responsible for developing and maintaining backend services for Savings & Investment area, and used by Internet Bank, Mobile apps and internal CRM-applications. Our main priority now is to develop the next generation of our API-platform. You will be a part of a highly motivated and creative team that works in close collaboration with our product owners, architects and other teams within Technology. We have an open culture where we help each other and drive the development of our area together. The team has a strong interest in new technology, and you have great opportunity to develop in your role as a QA-specialist/Test Automation Engineer.
   

  

 Our backend tech stack consists of:
   

  

* .Net Core
* C#
* ReadyAPI/SoapUI
* Git (Hub)
* Azure DevOps
* OpenShift
* GCP


**To thrive in this role, we believe you have:**
* Worked as QA specialist/Test automation engineer for several years.
* Experience with testing backend services, APIs, integrations.
* A passion for quality assurance, test automation and TDD.
* Experience in working with different test tools is a must and experience in ReadyAPI is meriting.
* Experience in backend development.
* A personality who likes to be proactive with a strong sense of ownership and responsibility, someone who loves to work in a team and is eager to learn and share knowledge.
* A bachelor's degree in computer science or equivalent is required.
* You need to be able to understand Swedish, but you don’t need to speak it fluently.


**What we offer:**
* Opportunities to design and develop automated tests from scratch.
* A team of experts that will support you.
* Existing tools, processes and infrastructure.
* Work-life balance and flexibility.
* Friendly and welcoming culture.


**Do you want to be a part of SEB?**
 It is our fundamental belief that inclusion and diversity is crucial for our future success. We strive to have an inclusive, value-driven culture where employees feel valued, respected, and involved irrespective of who they are, what they believe or where they come from.
   

  

 Since we select candidates continuously, feel free to send in your application today, but no later than 3 of April 2026. If you have questions about the position, please contact Lena Fureman at lena.fureman@seb.se, but we will not process applications via email.
   

  

 Please be aware that our final candidates undergo background checks, a process that includes for example identity control, verification of qualifications, credit checks, company engagements and history of crime. In some cases, we also apply random drug checks. During employment, employees within SEB can also expect to undergo recurring background checks.
   

  

 SEB Sweden has a redeployment responsibility, why this position might be covered by internal redeployment
   

  

 Learn more about working at SEB on our Career website or through our Career podcasts.
   

  

 sebgroup.com/career
   

  

 bit.ly/SEBcareer

title: Technical Lead
description:
Enertek Group is partnering with a leading financial services firm, specialising in trading platforms and technology-driven investment solutions for institutional clients. As part of their continued growth and platform evolution, they are expanding their engineering leadership team.
 



 On their behalf, we are seeking an experienced
 **Tech Lead (Backend – Go)** 
 based in
 **Stockholm, Sweden** 
 to drive the development of a high-performance internal trading platform used across their business.
 



  





 This is a key leadership role, offering the opportunity to shape the architecture and delivery of scalable, low-latency systems within a fast-paced financial environment, while mentoring engineers and working closely with both technical and business stakeholders.
 



  





**Market Insight & Upcoming Opportunity** 




 As part of our ongoing engagement with clients across Sweden, particularly in Stockholm, we are currently seeing a strong and consistent demand for senior backend engineering leadership within financial services.
 



  





 While this specific role is expected to go live in the coming months, we are proactively engaging with high-calibre Tech Lead candidates in anticipation of upcoming hiring needs across our client base. This position reflects a broader trend we are observing, with multiple organisations seeking expertise in
 **Go-based, low-latency trading systems** 
 in the near term.
 



  





 We welcome conversations with candidates who are open to exploring new opportunities and would like to be considered as these roles formally open.
 



  





**Key Responsibilities** 



* Lead the design and development of backend services for a high-performance trading platform, with a strong focus on
 **Go (Golang)**
* Own the technical direction and architecture of backend systems, ensuring scalability, reliability, and low latency
* Take end-to-end ownership of critical features, from design through to production deployment
* Mentor and guide engineers, promoting best practices and high engineering standards
* Collaborate closely with platform, DevOps, and infrastructure teams on CI/CD, observability, and system performance
* Work directly with stakeholders, including trading, product, and business teams, to define and deliver technical solutions
* Design and build real-time systems and data pipelines (e.g. WebSockets, streaming architectures)
* Drive improvements in system observability using tools such as
 **Prometheus and Grafana**
* Oversee code quality through reviews, testing strategies, and development best practices
* Champion modern engineering practices, including automated testing and efficient development workflows
* Leverage
 **AI/LLM-assisted development tools** 
 to enhance productivity and innovation within the team



  





**Skills & Experience** 



* Proven experience as a
 **Tech Lead or Senior Backend Engineer** 
 within
 **financial services, trading, or low-latency environments**
* Strong expertise in
 **Go (Golang)** 
 for backend system development
* Solid experience designing and building
 **distributed, high-performance systems**
* Deep understanding of real-time data processing and messaging (e.g. WebSockets, event-driven systems)
* Experience with databases such as
 **Redis and MS SQL**
* Strong experience with
 **CI/CD pipelines** 
 (e.g. GitHub Actions)
* Hands-on experience with
 **Docker and Kubernetes**
* Familiarity with observability and monitoring tools such as
 **Prometheus, Grafana, and SonarQube**
* Strong testing mindset and experience with automated testing frameworks
* Experience working in
 **cross-functional teams** 
 , including DevOps and platform engineering
* Ability to engage directly with stakeholders and translate business requirements into technical solutions
* Exposure to
 **AI-assisted development tools** 
 (e.g. OpenAI, Anthropic) is highly desirable
* Excellent communication skills in English



  





**Location Requirements** 



* Candidates must be
 **based in Sweden** 
 , with a strong preference for those located in
 **Stockholm**
* Hybrid working model available



  





**What We Offer** 



* Permanent full-time position based in Stockholm
* Competitive salary and comprehensive benefits package
* Opportunity to lead the development of cutting-edge trading systems in a high-impact financial environment
* Access to the latest AI development tools and technologies
* Collaborative, high-performing engineering culture with strong career progression opportunities

title: Senior Software Engineer – Tieto Banktech (m/f/d)
description:
**Job Description**
 Tieto Banktech powers some of the Nordics’ most trusted banking systems with secure, reliable, and scalable solutions. Using automation, data, AI, and cloud, supported by hands-on guidance, we help banks modernize their core, strengthen trust, and deliver greater impact across the banking experience.
   

  

 We are seeking a Senior Software Engineer who is both hands-on and strategic, to play a pivotal role in modernizing and leading the development of our Equity post-trade platform. Working closely with the Product Manager, you will spend the majority of your time coding while shaping the architecture, mentoring the team, and driving the transition from legacy monolithic systems to modular, maintainable, and scalable solutions.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities**
* Hands-on Development: Architect, design, and implement robust solutions, with a focus on RESTful APIs, microservices, and domain-driven design.
* Legacy Modernization: Lead the refactoring of monolithic, DB-driven systems, lifting business logic out of stored procedures into modular and maintainable components.
* Technical Leadership: Mentor and guide a distributed engineering team (Stockholm & Pune), promoting best practices, clean code, and technical excellence.
* Architecture Strategy: Collaborate with Product Manager to define and implement a technical roadmap aligned with business goals.
* Quality & Resilience: Drive initiatives to improve code quality, reliability, and maintainability in complex systems.
* Business Collaboration: Translate complex business workflows into technical solutions while balancing speed, scalability, and risk.


**Required Skills & Competencies**
* Strong programming experience in SQL, C#, and .NET, with a track record of hands-on coding in production systems.
* Proven experience in API design, microservices architecture, and domain-driven design.
* Track record of driving technical transformation in legacy systems.
* Demonstrated ability to modernize legacy, DB-heavy systems, refactor monolithic codebases, and implement modular architectures.
* Experience leading and mentoring distributed teams, fostering knowledge sharing and technical growth.
* Familiarity with CI/CD pipelines, cloud platforms (Azure), Git, and DevOps practices.
* Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills.


**Preferred Qualifications**
* Master’s degree in Computer Science or a related field.
* Experience in Equity post-trade or Capital Markets systems.
* Understanding of equity trading workflows and complex business scenarios.
* Minimum 7 years of experience working on enterprise-level solutions.
* Hands-on experience with containerization technologies (Docker/Kubernetes) and cloud-native architectures.
* Experience working in a product organization with strong collaboration between tech and business.


**We Offer**
 We offer a unique opportunity to shape the architecture of a critical banking platform, work on meaningful projects, and influence a growing, distributed team. You will have the freedom to be hands-on while also driving modernization, supported by an open culture, flexible hybrid work model, and a focus on continuous learning.
   

  

 Interested?
   

  

 We look forward to receiving your application! Interviews will be held continuously, so we encourage you to apply today. For more information or questions, please contact our Talent Acquisition team at recruitmentinbanking@tieto.com.
   

  

 We perform background checks on all final candidates.
   

  

 Tieto declines calls from recruitment companies.
   

  

 Additional Information
   

  

 At Tieto, we believe in the power of diversity, equity, and inclusion. We encourage applicants of all backgrounds, genders (m/f/d), and walks of life to join our team, as we believe that this fosters an inspiring workplace and fuels innovation. Our commitment to openness, trust, and diversity is at the heart of our mission to create digital futures that benefit businesses, societies, and humanity.
   

  

 Diversity, equity and inclusion (tietoevry.com)

title: Mainframe Tester
description:
**Role: Mainframe Tester** 




**Location: Stockholm, Sweden** 




**Duration: 12 months** 




  




1. Understand and review requirements for payment products
2. Define test scenarios and expected outcomes, fail scenarios
3. Define and understand issue tracking and documentation
4. Understand system documentation and architect descriptions
5. End-to-end test planning and coordination for projects
6. Communication with external/internal stakeholders, e.g. program and epic owners, on test related matters
7. Coordinate execution of manual tests with system testers

title: Backend Engineer, Healthtech
description:
Join us At Platform24 you will build software for automation of national medical triage, remote patient monitoring and surgical planning, solving real-world healthcare challenges at scale for both doctors and patients.
 



  





**How we work** 




 Our success is rooted in collaborative teams that own their domain. You’ll work closely with a mix of medical doctors, frontend and backend engineers, interaction designers, a product lead, and an engineering manager. We operate with a high level of trust and clear end-to-end ownership — from UI to backend services and DevOps — in an environment where every voice is heard, and contributions have a real impact on clinicians' and patients' lives.
 



  





**Your role** 




 You’ll be at the heart of healthcare innovation, actively designing, building, and deploying new features. Beyond writing code, you’ll contribute to architectural decisions and technology choices. A key part of the role is working closely with customers and end users and communicating complex technical topics clearly and thoughtfully.
 



  





**Our tech stack:** 



* Primarily Java 21, but also a mix of TS and Python
* Primarily Spring Boot, but also a mix of Quarkus and Micronaut
* Mix of Hibernate / JPA and JOOQ SQL
* We run private cloud Kubernetes clusters for public healthcare customers and AWS & Azure for private ones.
* Microservices Architecture with high team autonomy
* Generous tiers for AI tooling of your choice: Claude Code, Gemini, Codex, etc



  





**What We Offer:** 



* Impact at scale: You’ll take part in the international expansion of digital healthcare systems. We are the market leader in Sweden and are actively growing our solution in other countries like Denmark,
 **the** 
 Netherlands, and other to be announced markets.
* AI Forefront: You’ll shape our LLM production usage as we are currently deploying and evaluating different models to aid our triage engine.
* Stockholm's Most Scenic Office: Enjoy our beautiful location in Münchenbryggeriet, offering a stunning view of Riddarfjärden. Our office perks include a great lunch culture and a private free gym, both of which support a healthy work-life balance.
* Hybrid Work Setup, where you meet your team members in the office 2-3 days per week.
* Culture & Balance: We support work-life balance through flexible hours, bi-weekly Demo & Beers sessions (beer is optional :)), and a unique benefit: five paid volunteer days per year, dedicated to causes you care about.



  





**Apply:** 




 If you believe we're the right workplace for you and you're the right fit for us, submit your application today! We conduct ongoing interviews, so we encourage you to apply soon..
 



  





 We are an equal-opportunity employer and value diversity. All employment decisions are based on qualifications, merit, and business needs.
 



  





 This role is a full-time employment with Platform24. We do not offer contractor and/or B2B types of engagements.

title: Software Engineer, Sustaining Engineering
description:
Canonical is a leading provider of open source software and operating systems to the global enterprise and technology markets. Our platform, Ubuntu, is very widely used in breakthrough enterprise initiatives such as public cloud, data science, AI, engineering innovation, and IoT. Our customers include the world's leading public cloud and silicon providers, and industry leaders in many sectors. The company is a pioneer of global distributed collaboration, with 1200+ colleagues in 75+ countries and very few office-based roles. Teams meet two to four times yearly in person, in interesting locations around the world, to align on strategy and execution.
   

  

 The company is founder-led, profitable, and growing.
   

  

 We are hiring a
 **Software Engineer, Sustaining Engineering** 
 for ...
   

  

* a fast-paced engineering role in Linux-based software-defined infrastructure and applications, covering all layers of the stack, including bare metal, virtualization (KVM) and containerization (Docker/LXC/LXD), storage (Ceph and Linux filesystems), networking (OVS, OVN and Core networking), up to OpenStack and Kubernetes, and the open source applications running on top of them. It will challenge you to show the breadth of engineering skill needed to work on almost any aspect of Ubuntu and the open source things people run with it.


 This role is an opportunity for a technologist with a passion for Linux and open source to build a career with Canonical and drive success for our customers, community and the company. If you have an affinity for open source development, great communication skills, and a passion for troubleshooting and fixing issues in technology used by millions across the world, then you will enjoy working with some of the best people in the industry at Canonical.
   

  

**Location:** 
 This is a remote role, we have teams in all time zones.
   

  

 This role deals with critical issues in the open source stack that require software engineering for upstream bug fixes. Our engineers have to be able to work productively at any level of the stack above the kernel, in a wide range of languages, to understand and address the software issues at hand. Our group is critical to the success of our enterprise customers, partners and Ubuntu itself.
   

  

 You will help with troubleshooting and driving issues to resolution with workarounds, guidance, and bug fixes to be released upstream and in Ubuntu.
   

  

 This role entails
   

  

* Resolving complex customer problems related to Ubuntu, Kernel, Ceph, OpenStack, or Kubernetes and other open source software
* Maintaining a close working relationship with Canonical's Field, Support and product engineering teams
* Participating in upstream communities
* Developing bug fixes, backporting patches, and working with upstream for inclusion
* Reviewing code produced by other engineers
* Demonstrating good judgement in technical methods and techniques
* Prioritizing work and managing your time effectively against those priorities
* Participating in team discussions to improve processes, tools, and documentation
* Maintaining clear, technical and concise communications
* Working from home and travel internationally up to 10% of work time for team meetings, events and conferences


 What we are looking for in you
   

  

* Professional experience as a software engineer
* Background in Computer Science, STEM or similar
* **Strong** 
 experience with Linux, and at least one of the following:
+ Ceph, OpenStack, Kubernetes or other cloud technologies

* Strong development-level experience with at least one of Python, Go, C or C++ on Linux
* Ability to troubleshoot with debugging tools like gdb
* Familiarity with git source code repositories and branches
* An exceptional academic track record
* Willingness to travel up to 4 times a year for internal events


 Nice-to-have skills
   

  

* You love technology and working with brilliant people
* You are curious, flexible, articulate, and accountable
* You value soft skills and are passionate, enterprising, thoughtful, and self-motivated
* You have interest in, and experience with most of the following: Ubuntu Linux - kernel or userspace, Kubernetes, OpenStack, Ceph, QEMU/KVM, LXC/LXD, Python, Go, C, Postgresql, Mongo, Debian packaging, distributed systems


 What we offer colleagues
   

  

 We consider geographical location, experience, and performance in shaping compensation worldwide. We revisit compensation annually (and more often for graduates and associates) to ensure we recognize outstanding performance. In addition to base pay, we offer a performance-driven annual bonus. We provide all team members with additional benefits, which reflect our values and ideals. We balance our programs to meet local needs and ensure fairness globally.
   

  

* Distributed work environment with twice-yearly team sprints in person - we've been working remotely since 2004!
* Personal learning and development budget of USD 2,000 per year
* Annual compensation review
* Recognition rewards
* Annual holiday leave
* Maternity and paternity leave
* Employee Assistance Programme
* Opportunity to travel to new locations to meet colleagues from your team and others
* Priority Pass for travel and travel upgrades for long haul company events


**About Canonical**
 Canonical is a pioneering tech firm at the forefront of the global move to open source. As the company that publishes Ubuntu, one of the most important open-source projects and the platform for AI, IoT, and the cloud, we are changing the world of software. We recruit on a global basis and set a very high standard for people joining the company. We expect excellence; in order to succeed, we need to be the best at what we do. Most colleagues at Canonical have worked from home since our inception in 2004. Working here is a step into the future and will challenge you to think differently, work smarter, learn new skills, and raise your game.
   

  

 Canonical is an equal opportunity employer
   

  

 We are proud to foster a workplace free from discrimination. Diversity of experience, perspectives, and background create a better work environment and better products. Whatever your identity, we will give your application fair consideration.

description:
